title: SonarQube Server 2025.3 | Documentation
content:
# SonarQube Server 2025.3 Documentation

## [What is SonarQube Server?](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/#what-is-sonarqube-server "What is SonarQube Server?")

SonarQube Server is an industry-standard on-premises automated code review and static analysis tool designed to detect coding issues in [30+ languages](https://rules.sonarsource.com/ "30+ languages"), [frameworks, and IaC platforms](https://rules.sonarsource.com/ "frameworks, and IaC platforms"). By integrating directly with your [CI pipeline](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/analyzing-source-code/ci-integration/overview/ "CI pipeline") or on one of our supported DevOps platforms, your code is checked against an extensive set of rules that cover many attributes of code, such as maintainability, reliability, and security issues on each merge/pull request.

As a core element of the Sonar solution, SonarQube Server completes the analysis loop to help you deliver clean code that meets high-quality standards.

Please see the [Try out SonarQube Server](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/try-out-sonarqube/ "Try out SonarQube Server") page to learn how to get started. For a Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) cloud-based tool, see [SonarQube Cloud](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/ " SonarQube Cloud").

## [The approach to Clean Code](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/#clean-code "The approach to Clean Code")

[Clean Code](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/user-guide/clean-code/introduction/ "Clean Code") is the standard for all code that results in secure, reliable, and maintainable software therefore, writing clean code is essential to maintaining a healthy codebase. This applies to all code: source code, test code, infrastructure as code, glue code, scripts, and more.

Sonar's [Clean as You Code](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/user-guide/clean-as-you-code/introduction/ "Clean as You Code") approach is a software development practice based on the principle that all new code (whether added or recently modified) should adhere to quality standards. SonarQube for IDE achieves this by providing automated code reviews that alert you to potential issues within your new code. This helps you maintain high standards and focus on code quality, ultimately leading to a healthier codebase over time.

SonarQube Server comes with a built-in [quality profile](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/quality-standards-administration/managing-quality-profiles/understanding-quality-profiles/ "quality profile") designed for each supported language, called the **Sonar Way** profile. The **Sonar way** activates a set of rules that should be applicable to most projects and is a starting point to help you implement clean code practices in your organization.

## [The Sonar solution](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/#the-sonar-solution "The Sonar solution")

Sonar products are designed to help you achieve a state of Clean Code. By linking [SonarQube for IDE](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarlint/ "SonarQube for IDE") with [SonarQube Cloud](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/ "SonarQube Cloud") or SonarQube Server, the automated code analysis and reviews are performed at every stage of the development process. We call this the Sonar solution. This means your project settings, new code definitions, and quality profiles are applied locally to an analysis in the IDE. Your project settings, new code definitions, and the quality profiles managed in SonarQube (Server, Cloud) are applied locally to an analysis in the IDE.

- [SonarQube for IDE](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarlint/ "SonarQube for IDE") brings automated code reviews directly into your development environment, helping you catch issues as you write code. By providing immediate feedback, it enables engineers to identify and fix problems before they even commit, ensuring cleaner, higher-quality code from the start.
- [SonarQube Server](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/analyzing-source-code/pull-request-analysis/introduction/ "SonarQube Server") and [SonarQube Cloud](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/improving/pull-request-analysis/ "SonarQube Cloud") deliver powerful static code analysis by thoroughly reviewing each pull request before it’s merged. This proactive approach adds an essential layer of protection, ensuring code quality and preventing issues from entering your codebase.
- Finally, [SonarQube Server](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/quality-standards-administration/managing-quality-gates/introduction-to-quality-gates/ "SonarQube Server") and [SonarQube Cloud](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/improving/quality-gates/ "SonarQube Cloud") seamlessly integrate into your CI/CD pipeline, analyzing code on every build. By leveraging [quality profiles](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/quality-standards-administration/managing-quality-profiles/understanding-quality-profiles/ "quality profiles"), and [quality gates](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/quality-standards-administration/managing-quality-gates/introduction-to-quality-gates/ "quality gates"), they automatically block code with issues from being released to production, ensuring only maintainable, reliable, and secure code makes it through.

The Sonar solution helps you incorporate the [Clean as You Code](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/user-guide/clean-as-you-code/introduction/ "Clean as You Code") methodology by helping engineers pay attention to new code. Focusing on writing new, clean code during development ensures that all code released for production will be incrementally improved over time.

## [Connected Mode](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/#connected-mode "Connected Mode")

Connected Mode joins SonarQube Server with SonarQube for IDE to deliver the full Sonar solution. While in Connected Mode, SonarQube Server sends notifications to SonarQube for IDE when a quality gate changes or a new issue is assigned to the user. Smart notifications can be enabled or disabled from the SonarQube for IDE interface while creating or editing the connection settings. Additionally, SonarQube for IDE helps engineers focus on writing clean code by using the new code definition from the server. Be sure to check out all of the [Connected Mode benefits](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/user-guide/connected-mode/ "Connected Mode benefits").

## [Getting started](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/#getting-started "Getting started")

Now that you've heard about how [SonarQube Server](https://www.sonarsource.com/products/sonarqube/ "SonarQube Server") can help you write clean code, you are ready to try out SonarQube Server for yourself. You can run a local non-production instance of SonarQube Server and the initial project analysis. Installing a local instance gets you up and running quickly, so you can experience SonarQube Server firsthand. Then, when you're ready to set up SonarQube Server in production, you'll need to [install the server](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/setup-and-upgrade/install-the-server/introduction/ "install the server") before configuring your first code analysis.

The [Analyzing source code](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/analyzing-source-code/overview/ "Analyzing source code") section explains how to connect your scanner to your CI pipeline and provides instructions for analyzing your project’s branches and pull requests.

Here is a page with everything you need to [Try out SonarQube Server](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/try-out-sonarqube/ "Try out SonarQube Server").

## [Learn more](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/#learn-more "Learn more")

Check out the entire suite of Sonar products: [SonarQube Server](https://www.sonarsource.com/products/sonarqube/ "SonarQube Server"), [SonarQube Cloud](https://www.sonarsource.com/products/sonarcloud/ "SonarQube Cloud"), and [SonarQube for IDE](https://www.sonarsource.com/products/sonarlint/ "SonarQube for IDE").

Then, have a look at how to fix issues detected by SonarQube for [IntelliJ](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/intellij/using/fixing-issues/ "IntelliJ"), [Visual Studio](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/visual-studio/using/fixing-issues/ "Visual Studio"), [VS Code](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/vs-code/using/fixing-issues/ "VS Code"), and [Eclipse](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/eclipse/using/fixing-issues/ "Eclipse") when combined with [SonarQube Server](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/user-guide/issues/introduction/ "SonarQube Server") and [SonarQube Cloud](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/managing-your-projects/issues/introduction/ "SonarQube Cloud"), and browse a full list of [Sonar Rules and Rule Descriptions](http://rules.sonarsource.com/ "Sonar Rules and Rule Descriptions") available for static code analysis.

### [More getting started resources](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/#more-getting-started-resources "More getting started resources")

- [Server installation and setup](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/setup-and-upgrade/overview/ "Server installation and setup")
- [How to create and import projects](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/project-administration/creating-and-importing-projects/ "How to create and import projects")
- [How to administer quality profiles](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/quality-standards-administration/managing-quality-profiles/introduction/ "How to administer quality profiles")
- [How to set up portfolios](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/project-administration/managing-portfolios/ "How to set up portfolios")

## [Staying connected](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/#staying-connected "Staying connected")

Use the following links to follow SonarQube Server behind the scenes:

- [Source code](https://github.com/SonarSource "Source code")
- [Issue tracker Jira](https://jira.sonarsource.com/ "Issue tracker Jira")

And if you need help, visit our [online community](https://community.sonarsource.com/c/sq/10 "online community") to search for answers and reach out with questions!
